merger_description:
Hillhouse Investment Management, Ltd. (**Hillhouse**) proposes to acquire 100% of the issued shares in MGI Dobbyn Carafa through Ascentium AU Oxygen Holdings Limited, which is wholly and directly owned by Hillhouse (the **Acquisition**).

Hillhouse is a global alternative investment manager that deploys capital across multiple strategies, including public equities, private equity, private credit, and real assets. Through its private equity arm, Hillhouse invests in long-term global opportunities across the business services, healthcare, consumer, and industrials sectors.

Relevant to the Acquisition, Hillhouse’s investments include:

* In.Corp Advisory (AU) Pty Ltd (**Incorp**): Incorp provides standardised bookkeeping, basic accounting, compliance and company secretarial services, focusing on small enterprises, sole proprietors and small business owners. InCorp’s connected entity, InCorp Audit & Assurance Pty Ltd, provides auditing and assurance services.
* Oxygen Private Clients Pty Ltd (**Oxygen**): Oxygen is an Australian tax and advisory firm providing specialised compliance and advisory services to family offices, family groups, high-net-worth-individuals, owner-managed businesses, sole traders and self-managed super funds.
* Lumiere Private Advisory Pty Ltd (**Lumiere**): Lumiere advises on superannuation matters particularly in relation to self-managed superfunds and offers services including income tax returns, accounts and lodgement with the ATO, as well as estate planning and asset succession implications.

MGI Dobbyn Carafa Pty Ltd (**MGI Dobbyn Carafa**) is a Melbourne-based company that provides accounting, tax and corporate advisory services to corporate clients in Australia consisting of accounting and tax compliance, tax consulting, corporate advisory, superannuation, and audit and assurance services.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
Ascentium AU Oxygen Holdings Limited, which is wholly and directly
owned by Hillhouse Investment Management, Ltd. (Hillhouse),
applied for a notification waiver in respect of its proposed
acquisition of 100% of the issued shares in MGI Dobbyn Carafa Pty
Ltd (MGI Dobbyn Carafa), as described in the transaction documents
provided as part of the application (the Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.

Hillhouse is a global alternative investment manager that deploys
capital across multiple strategies, including public equities, private
equity, private credit, and real assets. Through its private equity arm,
Hillhouse invests in long-term global opportunities across the
business services, healthcare, consumer, and industrials sectors.
Relevantly, Hillhouse’s investments include Australian accounting,
tax, advisory, corporation and self-managed super services through
In.Corp Advisory (AU) Pty Ltd, Oxygen Private Clients Pty Ltd and
Lumiere Private Advisory Pty Ltd.
MGI Dobbyn Carafa is a Melbourne-based company that provides
accounting, tax and corporate advisory services to corporate clients
in Australia consisting of accounting and tax compliance, tax
consulting, corporate advisory, superannuation, and audit and
assurance services.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered
the information provided with the notification waiver application and
had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition
and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).
Based on the information provided in the application, the ACCC
considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material
lessening of competition. In particular:
a. there is a small horizontal overlap between Hillhouse
(including its connected entities) and MGI Dobbyn Carafa in
the supply of accounting, tax, and corporate advisory
services in Melbourne and Australia
b. there are alternative providers of accounting, tax, and other
corporate advisory services in Melbourne and Australia.
The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the
Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds
determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.
While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely
to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to
arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required
to be notified.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.
